name: Marine War Risk Insurance
summary: Day 71 Saturday. **AWRP UNCHANGED AT ~0.8-1% H&M / 7-DAY BASELINE — INSURERS HAVE NOT BOUGHT THE MAY 5 PAUSE AS DURABLE.** Trigger 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' is at Day 3 of 10 with no movement — failing. AWRP 6-8x pre-war 0.10-0.15% norm. **OPERATIONAL DETERIORATION REINFORCED:** Hormuz transit volume ZERO May 7 (down from 7 May 6); 1,500 commercial vessels anchored or holding inside the Persian Gulf (CONFIRMED — Lloyd's List Intelligence via CNN). CENTCOM disclosed >70 tankers held under blockade + 20 warships, 200 aircraft, 15,000 troops enforcing. M/T Sea Star III + M/T Sevda disabled May 8 (after M/T Hasna May 7) = second day of US precision-munition strikes against Iranian-flagged tankers attempting to break the blockade. **The single most reliable physical-pricing leading indicator continues to refute the diplomatic-track durability hypothesis.** VLCC TD3C ~$100K/day baseline per Lloyd's List; FRO +3.6% 1D / +13.5% 1M / +40.1% 3M. War risk surcharge differential: US/UK/Israeli vessels charged 3x non-aligned (Lloyd's List). Joint War Committee Persian Gulf 'high-risk' designation MAINTAINED. The paper-vs-physical bifurcation now operates inside the trinary regime: paper markets (S&P record, defense complex unwinding) pricing the diplomatic track; physical (zero transits, 1,500-vessel standstill, AWRP at 6-8x norm) pricing the operational reality. France Charles de Gaulle CSG remains pre-positioned Red Sea / Gulf of Aden — UNCHANGED, no operational deployment to Hormuz.

current.cover_current: $10-14M per Persian Gulf voyage; broad rates 0.8-1.5%; US/UK/Israeli flags 3x; some easing to 0.35-0.45% on broader MEG routes. 329 vessels = ~$352B coverage gap.

active_triggers.condition: Lloyd's JWC removes Arabian Gulf from conflict zone designation
AWRP normalization toward 0.5% within 10 sessions (by 2026-05-16) = pause priced as durable de-escalation
active_triggers.reframed_from: AWRP 5%+ within 5 sessions (Project Freedom effectiveness verification) — obsoleted by May 5 pause

recent_signals.headline: AWRP AT ~0.8-1% H&M / 7-DAY BASELINE — INSURERS NOT YET PRICING MOU FRAMEWORK AS DURABLE; DAY 1-2 OF 'AWRP NORMALIZATION TOWARD 0.5% WITHIN 10 SESSIONS' TRIGGER (BY MAY 16). VLCC TD3C ~$420-474K/day TCE sustained; STEEPEST 1-DAY DROP IN 5+ YEARS ON MOU NEWS ('LEAD BALLOON' — Lloyd's List). Hormuz transits 5-6/day vs 138 norm. CMA CGM San Antonio struck May 5 by cruise missile keeps kinetic-tail risk live (CBS attribution to Iran, IRGC has NOT claimed). Paper-vs-physical bifurcation IS the structural intelligence.
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED operational data multi-source; CMA CGM strike = CONFIRMED action, CLAIMED Iran attribution
